title: Pharmacokinetics Engine — ADME Computation
description: Pharmacokinetic computations: AUC (linear & log-linear trapezoidal), clearance, volume of distribution, steady-state concentration (Css = F·Dose/CL·τ), time to steady state (4.5 half-lives), Michaelis-Menten saturation kinetics, half-life, bioavailability. Pure computation from UACA L1 atoms — no external API calls. Source: nv.shared.core.atoms.pk.

tools.name: pk-auc
tools.description: Calculate Area Under the Concentration-Time Curve using linear or log-linear trapezoidal method. AUC quantifies total drug exposure. Linear: Σ (C1+C2)/2 × Δt. Log-linear preferred for elimination phase.

tools.name: pk-clearance
tools.description: Calculate systemic clearance from dose and AUC. CL = (F × Dose) / AUC. For IV: F = 1.0. For oral: F < 1.0 reflects first-pass metabolism.

parameters.name: bioavailability
parameters.description: Fraction absorbed F (0-1, default 1.0 for IV)

tools.name: pk-steady-state
tools.description: Calculate average steady-state concentration. Css = (F·Dose)/(CL·τ). Reached after ~4–5 half-lives. Used to verify therapeutic window compliance.

tools.name: pk-half-life
tools.description: Calculate elimination half-life from clearance and Vd. t½ = (0.693 × Vd) / CL. Clinically: ~4–5 half-lives to reach steady state or eliminate drug.

tools.name: pk-michaelis-menten
tools.description: Calculate Michaelis-Menten saturation kinetics. Rate = (Vmax × [S]) / (Km + [S]). When [S] >> Km: zero-order (capacity-limited). Models nonlinear elimination (ethanol, phenytoin).
